Drift hygiene: prose that ages vs. gates that don't

Three fixes from the 2026-07-18 drift audit. Skill prose carries claims that silently rot ("see #1847 for the migration" — it shipped months ago), and hooks.json carried its whole changelog inside one JSON string. This PR fixes the found rot and adds the gate that keeps it fixed.

2
Stale claims fixed
1,694
Files swept weekly
7.7KB
Changelog out of JSON
0
Stale claims left

1. hooks.json description: 7,769 chars to one stamped line

Before

"description": "OrchestKit hooks - 150 global + 46
agent-scoped + 22 skill-scoped = 218 total hooks
(216 -> 218: P3-A3 2026-07-15 — registered 2
never-listened CC 2.1.208 events as OBSERVER-ONLY
telemetry hooks (never block, never modify):
prompt/prompt-expansion-observer ...
[7,500 more characters of embedded changelog:
#2590, #2376, #959, #debt, #2371, #2335, M168
Phase 4, Phase 3, Phase 2, #1884, M141, M138,
M119, M104, M121, M126, M130, M140 ...]"

After

"description": "OrchestKit hooks - 150 global +
46 agent-scoped + 22 skill-scoped = 218 total
hooks. Registry history lives in README.md
(Registry changelog) - do not append changelog
prose here, only re-stamp the counts."

History preserved verbatim in src/hooks/README.md, split into 13 readable entries. The one code consumer (check-docs-facts.mjs regex = N total hooks) still parses.

2. The stale claims the sweep caught (and this PR fixes)

Rotted

brainstorm/SKILL.md:418
"...See #1847 for the migration of ork's
existing notification hooks."
(#1847 CLOSED; code shipped in
notification/desktop.ts + sound.ts)

code-review-playbook/SKILL.md:216
"...See #1940 for the overlap analysis..."
(#1940 CLOSED as cc-stale, no conclusion)

Fixed

brainstorm/SKILL.md
"Ork's notification hooks (notification/desktop,
notification/sound) already emit via
terminalSequence (#1847, shipped)."

code-review-playbook/SKILL.md
sentence dropped — the preceding line already
states the real guidance; no conclusion to cite

3. Try the gate: does a line read as forward-looking?

Sweep: scripts/audit-skill-claims.mjs — 1,694 markdown files under src/skills + src/agents; a line is flagged only when it phrases an issue as pending AND every issue it cites is CLOSED. Workflow: .github/workflows/skill-claims-audit.yml, Mondays, maintains one rolling report issue (comments, never closes — repo convention). Companion to the docs-site facts gate that ended the MDX count rot.
